name: nwon-baseline

metadata.name: nwon_baseline
metadata.summary: Python Code that is used in several projects

documentation:
# NWON Python baseline package

This package provides some basic python functions that can be used across several projects.

The dependencies of the project are kept to a minimum in order to prevent version conflicts with other projects.

Package is meant for internal use at [NWON](https://nwon.de) as breaking changes may occur on version changes. This may change at some point but not for now 😇.

## Development Setup

We recommend developing using poetry. 

This are the steps to setup the project with a local virtual environment:

1. Tell poetry to create dependencies in a `.venv` folder withing the project: `poetry config virtualenvs.in-project true`
1. Create a virtual environment using the local python version: `poetry env use $(cat .python-version)`
1. Install dependencies: `poetry install`

## Prepare Package

As we want to include types with the package it is not as straight forward as just calling `poetry build` 😥.

We need to:

1. Clean dist folder
1. Bump up the version of the package
1. Build the package

Luckily we provide a script for doing all of this `python scripts/prepare.py patch`. Alternatively you can run the script in a poetry context `poetry run prepare patch`. The argument at the end defines whether you want a `patch`, `minor` or `major` version bump.

The final zipped data ends up in the `dist` folder.

## Publish Package

Before publishing the package we need to:

1. Add test PyPi repository: `poetry config repositories.testpypi https://test.pypi.org/legacy/`
2. Publish the package to the test repository: `poetry publish -r testpypi`
3. Test package: `pip install --index-url https://test.pypi.org/simple/ nwon_baseline`

If everything works fine publish the package via `poetry publish`.

functions.name: nwon_baseline.shell_helper.boolean_environment_value.boolean_environment_value
documentation: Parses an environment variable to boolean

functions.name: nwon_baseline.shell_helper.get_environment_variable.get_environment_variable
documentation: Getting environment variable by key. Raises exception when variables is not set.

functions.name: nwon_baseline.file_helper.file.file_extension_from_url
documentation:
Returns the extensions of the file in an url including the do in the beginning.
E.g. www.test.de/file.jpeg?whatever=what would return .jpg

functions.name: nwon_baseline.file_helper.file.file_extension_from_path
documentation:
The final component's last suffix, if any.

This includes the leading period. For example: '.txt'

functions.name: nwon_baseline.file_helper.filename_from_url.filename_from_url
documentation:
Return basename corresponding to url.
\>>> print(url2filename('<http://example.com/path/to/file%C3%80?opt=1>'))
fileÀ
\>>> print(url2filename('<http://example.com/slash%2fname>')) # '/' in name
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
ValueError

functions.name: nwon_baseline.date_helper.time_differences.time_until
documentation:
Time from now until a given date_time.

Returned is a Tuple of (days, hours, minutes)

functions.name: nwon_baseline.date_helper.datetime.datetime_now
documentation: Returns the timezone aware current date time.

functions.name: nwon_baseline.date_helper.datetime.datetime_with_timezone
documentation: Attaches timezone information to datetime if none is already included

functions.name: nwon_baseline.pydantic.schema_from_pydantic_model.schema_from_pydantic_model
documentation: Gets a schema from a pydantic in a way that drf_spectacular can process it properly

functions.name: nwon_baseline.pydantic.save_pydantic_model_instance.save_pydantic_model_instance_as_json
documentation:
Saves the Pydantic Model instance as a json file.

Expects the file_path to end with '.json'.
If the suffix is not json we take the basename of the file_path and attach .json

functions.name: nwon_baseline.pydantic.save_pydantic_model_schema.save_pydantic_model_schema
documentation:
Saves the JSON schema for a Pydantic Model in a json file.

Expects the file_path to end with '.json'.
If the suffix is not json we take the basename of the file_path and attach .json

functions.name: nwon_baseline.import_helper.import_from_file.import_data_from_file
documentation:
Gets data from a file that is separated by some characters and arranged in one
dataset per line.

By defining the column names we now how much data you expect per line and can
provide better error messages. In the process some validation is done on the file

functions.name: nwon_baseline.unique_list.unique_list
documentation: Unifies items in a list.

functions.name: nwon_baseline.image_helper.image.bounding_box_coordinates_from_tuple
documentation: The box is expected to be a Tuple with coordinates (x1, y1, x2, y2)

functions.name: nwon_baseline.image_helper.image.bounding_box_tuple_from_coordinates
documentation: The box returned is a Tuple with coordinates (x1, y1, x2, y2)

functions.name: nwon_baseline.type_helper.enum_type_guard.enum_type_guard
documentation: Takes a string and an Enum class or a sub class of Enum and returns the typed value or None.

functions.name: nwon_baseline.type_helper.enum_value_list.enum_value_list
documentation: Returns a list with all the values in the enum

functions.name: nwon_baseline.type_helper.enum_value_list.enum_value_list_as_string
documentation: Returns a string with all the values of an Enum separated by a separator

functions.name: nwon_baseline.directory_helper.directory_interactions.clean_directory
documentation:
Cleans all files in a directory. Optionally also deletes all sub
directories including the containing files

functions.name: nwon_baseline.directory_helper.directory_content.file_names_in_directory
documentation:
Returns all file names (not the full path!) in a given directory.

Directories are filtered out

functions.name: nwon_baseline.directory_helper.directory_content.file_paths_in_directory
documentation:
Returns all file paths in a given directory.

Directories are filtered out.

Optionally it is possible to filter for certain file extensions.
File extensions can be defined with or without a dot in the beginning.

functions.name: nwon_baseline.directory_helper.directory_content.directory_names_in_directory
documentation:
Returns all directory names (not the full path!) in a given directory.

Files are filtered out.

functions.name: nwon_baseline.directory_helper.directory_content.directory_paths_in_directory
documentation:
Returns all directory paths in a given directory.

Files are filtered out.
